parameters.content:
### Generate and email PDF quotes from Airtable records

If you're managing quotes in Airtable and still copying data into a Google Doc or Word template every time, this saves you the hassle. Hit the webhook, and the workflow handles everything from there.

### How it works
1. You trigger the webhook with a record ID (either from an Airtable automation or a manual HTTP call)
2. It pulls the quote record from your Airtable base
3. A code node takes your business details and the line items, then builds a proper branded HTML quote with subtotals, tax, and a grand total
4. That HTML gets converted to a PDF via pdf.co (free tier works fine)
5. The PDF lands in your Google Drive folder and gets emailed to the client as an attachment
6. Finally, the Airtable record gets marked as "Sent" with the Drive link attached

### Setup
1. You need an Airtable base with a "Quotes" table. Columns: Client Name, Client Email, Line Items (long text field, one item per line like `Website Design | 1 | 2500`), Tax Rate, Notes, Status
2. Open the "Configure Settings" node and fill in your business name, email, address, and Drive folder ID
3. Connect your Airtable, Gmail, and Google Drive credentials
4. Grab a free API key from pdf.co and add it as an HTTP Header Auth credential
5. Activate and test with a real Airtable record ID

## 1. Trigger
Webhook receives a POST with the Airtable record ID. You can call this from an Airtable automation or any HTTP client.

## 2. Your settings
All the stuff you need to change is here. Business name, email, brand color, Drive folder, etc.

## 3. Pull the quote
Grabs the record from Airtable using the ID from the webhook.

## 4. Build the quote
This is where the magic happens. Parses line items, calculates totals, and builds a clean HTML document you can customize.

## 5. PDF, save, and send
Converts the HTML to PDF, uploads it to your Drive folder, and emails it to the client. The Airtable record gets updated with the link so you know it went out.

Hi {{ $('Build HTML Quote').first().json.clientName }},

Please find your quote ({{ $('Build HTML Quote').first().json.quoteRef }}) attached. The total is ${{ $('Build HTML Quote').first().json.grandTotal }}.

This quote is valid for 30 days. Please let us know if you have any questions.

Best regards,
{{ $('Build HTML Quote').first().json.businessName }}
